title: calceolaria pumila
intro: Calceolaria pumila, also known as the dwarf slipper flower, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Calceolaria genus and is native to the Andean region of South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Calceolaria pumila include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce inflammation. Calceolaria pumila can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
